content: Just as learning reflection in high school language arts requires moving beyond intuitive first responses to explicitly critique one’s own written drafts against external comprehension standards, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by adopting the structured reflective writing pedagogy that trains human learners to iterate on output. This means framing LLM self-reflection as a step-by-step guided writing workshop task, rather than an opaque internal model update, to elicit more consistent self-correction.
